scope.objective: Determine the fastest reliable path to generate CPU-WRF source-derived term savepoints for the selected h8-h14 dynamic divergence case.

verdict.fastest_reliable_path: Use a disposable instrumented copy of /home/user/src/wrf_pristine/WRF, because it has a current WRF v4.7.1 source tree and built CPU executables. Run the Case 3 d01/d02 forecast from 2026-05-01_18:00:00 to h10 with a small env-gated Fortran emitter that dumps selected d02 cell patches around existing dycore routine boundaries. Do not use the old external/wrf_savepoint_patch as-is; its hooks are empty and its build paths are stale.

expected_first_target.reason: h10 is the strongest selected h8-h14 divergence lead in the dynamic attribution plan.

verdict.source_truth_quality: source-derived WRF term savepoints are feasible, but the first implementation sprint must record build provenance and validate step/lead mapping before accepting h10 term data.

notable_existing_changes: dyn_em/solve_em.F has existing hardcoded idealized text dump probes.
phys/module_wrfgpu2_oracle.F exists and provides a raw-binary plus sidecar emission pattern for physics oracles.
physics driver files and generated registry files are modified or untracked.
wrf_source_paths.recommendation: Use only through a disposable copy/worktree for V0.14 instrumentation; do not patch this tree in place.

wrf_source_paths.evidence: Case 3 rsl.error.0000 contains source paths under this tree and reports WRF V4.7.1.
wrf_source_paths.recommendation: Use for provenance and source-diff comparison. It is not the fastest path because configure.wrf and main/wrf.exe are absent.
Do not use for this sprint unless a specific source comparison is needed.

usable_patterns.use: HDF5 savepoint read/write pattern with payload hashing and metadata_json.
Metadata conventions and schema validation pattern.
Comparison/reporting pattern only; not a WRF source truth generator.
Fortran raw binary plus sidecar emission idiom, env-gated outside repo src.

not_sufficient_as_oracle.reason: Extracts from wrfout and computes calc_coef_w in Python; not source-derived WRF dycore term truth.
References stale source/executable paths that are absent in this checkout.
Current harness is useful CPU scaffolding but does not produce per-RK/acoustic source-derived WRF term savepoints.

existing_scripts_and_tests.schema_gap: Existing VALID_OPERATORS does not include V0.14 term groups such as advection, diffusion, pressure_gradient, coriolis, and boundary_spec_relax. A proof-local schema/postprocessor can avoid repo src edits in the implementation sprint, or a separate approved src-changing sprint can extend the validation schema.

orchestration.reason: Best first hook layer for pre/post snapshots around RK, large-step, small-step, and boundary calls.

minimal_patch_strategy.patch_location: outside repo src, in a disposable WRF worktree/copy such as /mnt/data/wrf_gpu2/v014_same_state_wrf/WRF or /tmp/wrf_v014_same_state_savepoint/WRF
phase_0_marker_validation: Copy the Case 3 run directory to an output scratch directory.
Reduce run length to h10 or h10 plus one output in the copied namelist only.
Add a minimal env-gated marker dump in solve_em.F for d02 near h10.
Run a short or bounded job to verify grid%itimestep-to-lead mapping and d02 ownership before dumping many fields.
phase_1_boundary_snapshots: Add a compact Fortran emitter module modelled on the raw-binary/sidecar approach in phys/module_wrfgpu2_oracle.F.
Gate all emission by env vars and domain id so normal WRF execution is unchanged.

Dump selected cell patches before and after solve_em routine calls for rk_tendency, rk_addtend_dry, small_step_prep, calc_p_rho, advance_uv, advance_mu_t, advance_w, small_step_finish, and spec_bdyupdate.
Write raw f64 little-endian payloads plus JSON sidecars during WRF execution; convert to HDF5 or Zarr after the run to avoid Fortran/HDF5 build risk.
phase_2_inner_split_if_needed: If the first divergence appears inside rk_tendency, add snapshots in module_em.F around rhs_ph, advection, diffusion, horizontal_pressure_gradient, pg_buoy_w, and coriolis.
If the first divergence appears inside small-step, add snapshots in module_small_step_em.F around calc_p_rho, advance_uv, advance_mu_t, advance_w, and calc_coef_w.
If boundary/spec relaxation is implicated, add snapshots around share/module_bc.F spec_bdyupdate and set_physical_bc3d.
If physics source folding is implicated, add focused snapshots around module_physics_addtendc.F accumulation and first_rk_step_part1/part2 tendency handoff.

recommended_env_contract.WRFGPU2_SAMESTATE_ROOT: output root for raw payloads and sidecars
recommended_env_contract.WRFGPU2_SAMESTATE_START_STEP: first d02 step to dump; expected h10 mapping is near 6000 but must be verified
recommended_env_contract.WRFGPU2_SAMESTATE_ACOUSTIC_SUBSTEPS: first,last or explicit substep ids after marker validation

minimal_patch_strategy.why_not_full_domain_first: Full-domain arrays are safest diagnostically, but selected patches with halo 8 and all relevant stagger indices are faster, smaller, and aligned to the accepted localization plan. The marker run should prove indexing before accepting cropped data.

expected_savepoint_artifact_schema.preferred_container: HDF5 after postprocessing raw Fortran payloads

raw_runtime_payload.format: little-endian f64 stream plus JSON sidecar
raw_runtime_payload.reason: Minimizes changes to WRF build/linking and follows an existing local oracle pattern.

notes: Original run used 28 MPI tasks according to rsl.error.0000.
Current fastest available executable in /home/user/src/wrf_pristine/WRF appears serial, so walltime may be materially longer unless the copied tree is rebuilt dmpar.
No wrfrst files were found in the Case 3 launch directory, so h10 cannot be reached by restart without creating a new restart first.

risks.impact: Instrumented source run must start at 2026-05-01_18:00:00 and advance to h10/h8-h14; no direct h10 restart shortcut exists.
risks.mitigation: Run copied case only to h10 first, minimize history output, and optionally create wrfrst at h8/h10 for follow-up iterations.

risks.impact: Usable built tree is serial and dirty, while the truth run was 28-rank dmpar from a different historical source path.
risks.mitigation: Use a disposable copied tree, record git/diff/build metadata, and prefer a dmpar rebuild if the next sprint can spend build time.

risks.impact: Using it directly would produce no real source-derived term truth and may fail due to missing paths.
risks.mitigation: Treat it only as historical scaffolding; implement new env-gated hooks in the copied WRF tree.

risks.risk: Patch cropping can miss needed stencil or boundary context.
risks.impact: A local disagreement may be misattributed if halo/stagger handling is wrong.
risks.mitigation: Use halo >= 8, include native stagger patches, and validate one patch against wrfout/global indices before accepting term comparisons.

risks.impact: A dmpar run can duplicate or omit selected cells if each rank writes independently without ownership checks.
risks.mitigation: Emit rank/tile owner metadata and use unique per-rank files or a post-merge owner filter.

risks.risk: Nested-domain step mapping may be off by one.
risks.impact: Savepoints could target h10 plus or minus one d02 step.
risks.mitigation: Make the first marker dump record grid%itimestep, domain time, and wrfout-compatible Times before a full h10 emission.

risks.risk: Source-tendency folding is broad and physics-cadence dependent.
risks.impact: The initial hook layer may localize to source tendencies without explaining which physics contributor is responsible.
risks.mitigation: Only instrument physics_addtendc and first_rk_step_part1/part2 after the dycore boundary snapshots show this block is implicated.

risks.risk: Existing repo validation schema is too narrow.
risks.impact: V0.14 term groups do not fit current VALID_OPERATORS without src changes.
risks.mitigation: Use a proof-local schema/postprocessor for the implementation sprint, or start a separate approved schema-extension sprint.

next_implementation_sprint_contract_outl.title: V0.14 same-state WRF source term savepoint generator
next_implementation_sprint_contract_outl.objective: Generate CPU-WRF source-derived d02 h10 term savepoints for the selected 24 dynamic divergence cells, then compare them to the existing JAX CPU same-state term path without using JAX self-truth.
constraints: No edits to repo src unless a separate schema-extension contract is approved.
Do not patch /home/user/src/wrf_pristine/WRF in place; use a disposable copy/worktree.
All emitted truth must come from instrumented WRF source execution, not wrfout interpolation or Python recomputation.
implementation_steps: Create disposable WRF copy under /mnt/data/wrf_gpu2/v014_same_state_wrf/WRF or /tmp/wrf_v014_same_state_savepoint/WRF.
Record base source git/diff/build metadata and executable checksums.
Apply minimal env-gated Fortran emitter and solve_em.F hook patch.
Run marker validation to prove d02 h10 step mapping and patch indexing.
Run h10 emission for selected cells and required vertical/stagger contexts.
Postprocess raw payloads into HDF5 or manifest-backed arrays using v014-same-state-wrf-term-savepoint-v1 metadata.
Compare WRF source term savepoints against JAX CPU same-state terms at h10, then extend to h8/h12/h14 if h10 identifies a stable failure block.
acceptance_gates: Proof artifact records exact WRF source path, executable hash, patch hash, namelist/input checksums, and step/time mapping.
At least one marker field patch matches the corresponding h10 wrfout state at the same native indices within serialization tolerance.
Savepoints include pre/post data for the requested high-value term groups or explicitly document which group is postponed.
Comparison report identifies the first failing term group for h10 or states that no tested group reproduces the divergence.
No source-derived claim relies on JAX-vs-JAX self-comparison.
expected_proof_objects: raw/HDF5 savepoint payloads outside git under /mnt/data/wrf_gpu2/v014_same_state_wrf_savepoints/
next_implementation_sprint_contract_outl.recommended_first_decision: Approve a disposable WRF instrumentation sprint using /home/user/src/wrf_pristine/WRF as the build base, with optional dmpar rebuild if walltime/fidelity matters more than fastest first proof.

commands_run_summary: Read PROJECT_CONSTITUTION.md, AGENTS.md, V0140 handoff, same-state localization plan, sprint contract, and local project skills.
Inspected existing savepoint schema, IO, scripts, tests, and external WRF savepoint patch scaffolding.
Inspected WRF source/build candidates under /home/user/src/wrf_pristine/WRF, /home/user/src/canairy_meteo/Gen2/artifacts/wrf_src/WRF, /home/user/src/wrf_ideal_f7i/WRF, and stale old paths.
Inspected Case 3 launch/history directories, namelist, rsl logs, and NetCDF dimensions/metadata.
Extracted routine names/line numbers from WRF source files using ripgrep.
